news.t: Think the S&P 500 is too expensive? Check out these ETFs instead.

holdings.infoBox: JQUA is an equity ETF with a total of 294 individual holdings. The top holdings are Micron Technology stock at 3.06%, Advanced Micro Devices at 2.23%, Alphabet at 2.09%, Apple at 2.06%, and NVIDIA at 1.97%.

overview.description: The JPMorgan U.S. Quality Factor ETF (JQUA) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the JP Morgan US Quality Factor index. The fund selects companies from the Russell 1000 using three quality factors: profitability, earnings, and solvency. JQUA was launched on Nov 8, 2017 and is issued by JPMorgan Chase.
infoTable: JP Morgan US Quality Factor Index

data.title: Think the S&P 500 is too expensive? Check out these ETFs instead.
data.text: Some investors are worried that the stock market is set up for a tech-driven decline.

data.title: ETF Trends: Small Caps, Bitcoin, Defense
data.text: Cinthia Murphy says ETFs have been focused on small caps and defensive plays recently, though she points out that inflows are a lagging effect. She says there's been less of a rotation across sectors ...

data.text: Bryon Lake, head of Americas ETF client at J.P. Morgan Asset Management, and Todd Rosenbluth, senior director of ETF and mutual fund research at CFRA, break down how to play resurgent reopening trades...
